geographicSummary: District Six covers the West and Far West sides of Columbus, stretching from the West Broad Street corridor out toward Prairie Township, Galloway, and the Columbus border with Grove City. The district has the highest percentage of family households among the nine districts and a significant Hispanic population.

pointsOfInterest.name: Battelle Darby Creek Metro Park
pointsOfInterest.description: 7,358-acre park with forests, prairies, wetlands, reintroduced bison herds, nature center, and Osprey Lake.

pointsOfInterest.name: Prairie Township Community Center
pointsOfInterest.description: Community facility with indoor track, swimming pool, fitness center, and gymnasiums.

pointsOfInterest.name: Galloway Road Parkland
pointsOfInterest.description: 123-acre conservation area with walking trails and natural habitat preservation.

pointsOfInterest.name: Westland Library
pointsOfInterest.description: Columbus Metropolitan Library branch serving the Westland community with programs and meeting rooms.

pointsOfInterest.name: 5th by Northwest Area
pointsOfInterest.description: Neighborhood centered around West 5th Avenue with local businesses and community identity.

pointsOfInterest.name: Cypress Park
pointsOfInterest.description: Neighborhood park with soccer fields, baseball diamond, and playground facilities.

areaCommissions.name: 5th by Northwest Area Commission
areaCommissions.meetingSchedule: First Tuesday of each month, 7:00 PM

snapshots.source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 5-Year via Census API (backfill_2021.py)

data.data_note: Median income, rent, and home value are population-weighted averages of block-group medians — approximations, not true district medians. See /data/ for methodology.
